reason: Node 1.3.3 applies Node 1.2 (Weis-Shirokov) with K = S(A) and constraints f_j(psi) = psi(a_j) = beta_j, but does not verify or reference the verification that the hypotheses of Node 1.2 are satisfied. Specifically, Node 1.2 requires: (i) K is a compact convex set (or, per the definition Weis_Shirokov_theorem, a convex set in a locally convex space); (ii) the constraint functionals f_j are continuous affine (or more generally lower semicontinuous generalized affine maps). Node 1.3.1 is supposed to verify exactly these hypotheses, but Node 1.3.3 makes no reference to 1.3.1. Without this link, the application of Weis-Shirokov in 1.3.3 has an unjustified hypothesis gap. Note also that Node 1.3.1 itself has multiple open challenges (regarding Banach-Alaoglu for general *-algebras, the meaning of 'bounded' for general *-algebra elements, etc.), so this gap is not merely cosmetic -- it affects whether the argument is actually valid. FIX: Either (a) add a dependency on Node 1.3.1 and reference it ('by Node 1.3.1, the hypotheses of Node 1.2 hold'), or (b) inline the hypothesis verification within 1.3.3 itself.
